checks.reason: The corrected node is anchored to Agricola's 1556 De re metallica fire-assay description.
Fire assay uses fluxing, cupellation, lead collection, and acid parting rather than Paracelsian medical chemistry.
The assay workflow depends on weighing sampled material and recovered precious-metal beads.
The source-backed practice is used to determine whether metal-bearing rock is ore worth mining.
Paracelsian medical chemistry is outside the corrected metallurgical fire-assay scope.
The 1614 calibration-standard node is later than the 1556 fire-assay anchor.
Coin-mint administration is excluded from the corrected ore and precious-metal fire-assay workshop scope.
